steps.activity:
**Model message (Dutch)**

Subject: Vraag over bon

Goedemiddag,

Gisteren heb ik boodschappen gedaan bij uw winkel. Ik mis de bon in mijn tas. Kunt u een kopie mailen?

Met vriendelijke groet,
[jouw naam]

**Notice:** polite opening, short problem, clear request, closing.

**Later you will write:** Schrijf een mailtje naar de huisartsenpraktijk: vraag om een controleafspraak volgende week.
steps.teacher_notes: Optional slow audio for D/A.

steps.activity:
**Today's grammar — reflexives (body & health)**

**Rule:** *zich* changes with subject (*ik voel **me***, *je wast **je***).

**Patterns:**
• *Ik voel me niet goed.* · *Ik heb me verkouden.* · *Wast je handen alsjeblieft.*

**Produce:**
1. Say how you feel with *Ik voel me …*.
2. One sentence for a simple hygiene / health habit.

**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — ui / eu / ij:** round lips for *huis — leeuw*; *mijn* is one smooth glide.

**Language focus — patterns you can reuse**

**reflexive verbs (zich voelen, zich wassen)**
• NL: Ik voel me vandaag niet zo goed. / Je wast je handen voor het eten.
• EN: I don't feel so well today. / You wash your hands before eating.

**modal zullen for soft advice (Je zult moeten …) — recognition**
• NL: Gisteren had ik koorts en een zere keel. / Ik ben naar de huisartsenpraktijk gebeld.
• EN: Yesterday I had a fever and a sore throat. / I called the GP practice.

**past tense recognition (ik had pijn) for narratives**
• NL: De assistent vraagt of ik paracetamol verdraag. / Ik heb vandaag al drie liter water gedronken.
• EN: The assistant asks if I tolerate paracetamol. / I've already drunk three litres of water today.

**Try this:** pick one Dutch sentence above and change only the time or the person (ik → wij / vandaag → morgen), **or** do the “Produce” task from the grammar box above.
steps.teacher_notes: Max 2–3 patterns at A2.

steps.activity:
**1 — Label the parts:** opening / problem / request / closing in the model email.

**2 — Gap-fill:** Met vriendelijke ______, [naam].

**3 — Politeness:** choose softer Dutch: **Ik wil** → **Ik zou graag willen** in one sentence.

**4 — Combine:** Link with **want**: *Ik bel morgen, want ik ben vandaag druk.*

**5 — Shorten:** remove three words without losing the message.

**6 — Checklist tick:** subject line clear? closing present?

**7 — Your details:** replace [naam] and add one real detail from your life.

**8 — Read aloud:** read your draft slowly.
---

**All four skills — finish this lesson with these four micro-tasks**

🎧 **Listening:** Use TTS on this Dutch line (slow, then closer to natural): *Ik voel me vandaag niet zo goed.*

📖 **Reading:** Read silently, then say the **main idea in one English sentence**: *Ik voel me vandaag niet zo goed.*

✍️ **Writing:** Write **one new Dutch sentence** that uses **huisarts** and fits *Reflexive verbs (zich voelen, zich wassen)*.

🗣️ **Speaking:** Read your new sentence **aloud twice** — exaggerate stress the first time, normal the second.

*(If this lesson was already heavy on one skill, treat that skill as “extra reps” — the point is you always touch all four.)*

steps.activity:
Welcome! This lesson is built in small steps so you always know where you are.

**What you'll be able to do:** Complete a small real-world task about health & body (fix a message, choose replies, short role-card)

**Grammar thread today:** Perfectum (hebben/zijn + voltooid deelwoord) — you'll get explanations, examples, and production tasks in **Language focus** and **Guided practice**.

Take your time — you can repeat any step. Try to say at least one Dutch sentence out loud today.
steps.teacher_notes: Keep under ~1 min; reassure anxious learners.

steps.activity:
Let's wake up vocabulary about **Health & body**.

Words you might use today:
• **hoofdpijn**
• **keel**
• **neus**
• **verkouden**
• **apotheek**
• **recept**
• **paracetamol**
• **rust**

**Your turn (1–2 short sentences, Dutch or mixed):**
**Wat deed je gisteren?** — *What did you do yesterday?*

If you only know a few words, that's fine — start there.
steps.teacher_notes: Accept partial Dutch; encourage gestures.
steps.example_response: Gisteren voelde ik me niet zo goed en bleef ik thuis. Ik dronk veel thee en rustte 's middags even. 's Avonds ging het al wat beter.

steps.activity:
**Mini situation**

You get a short message that sounds a bit too direct in Dutch. Your job in the next steps is to choose a polite fix.

**Message (Dutch):**
Ik wil vandaag dokter. Zonder afspraak. Nu.

**I want the doctor today. Without an appointment. Now.**

We will upgrade the tone without changing the facts.
steps.teacher_notes: Optional slow audio for D/A.

steps.activity:
**Today's grammar — perfectum (short)**

**Rule:** *hebben/zijn + voltooid deelwoord* for completed experiences: *Ik **heb** gewerkt*, *Ik **ben** gekomen*.

**Patterns:**
• *Gisteren heb ik boodschappen gedaan.* · *Ben je op tijd gekomen?*

**Produce:**
1. One sentence: what you did yesterday (*gisteren heb ik …*).
2. One question with *heb je …* or *ben je …*.

**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — sch / sj:** *school* vs *sjabloon* — listen twice, then shadow.

**Language focus — patterns you can reuse**

**reflexive verbs (zich voelen, zich wassen)**
• NL: Je wast je handen voor het eten. / Ik voel me vandaag niet zo goed.
• EN: You wash your hands before eating. / I don't feel so well today.

**modal zullen for soft advice (Je zult moeten …) — recognition**
• NL: Ik ben naar de huisartsenpraktijk gebeld. / Gisteren had ik koorts en een zere keel.
• EN: I called the GP practice. / Yesterday I had a fever and a sore throat.

**past tense recognition (ik had pijn) for narratives**
• NL: Ik heb vandaag al drie liter water gedronken. / De assistent vraagt of ik paracetamol verdraag.
• EN: I've already drunk three litres of water today. / The assistant asks if I tolerate paracetamol.

**Try this:** pick one Dutch sentence above and change only the time or the person (ik → wij / vandaag → morgen), **or** do the “Produce” task from the grammar box above.
steps.teacher_notes: Max 2–3 patterns at A2.

steps.activity:
**1 — Tone:** which line feels too blunt for a landlord?

**2 — Rewrite opening** with **Goedemiddag** + **met vriendelijke groet** at the end.

**3 — Add one sentence** with the reason: *want de verwarming uitvalt bij kou.*

**4 — Choose best closing:** A) Groetjes B) Met vriendelijke groet C) Doei

**5 — Word order:** fix: *Ik wil graag een afspraak morgen.*

**6 — Scenario match:** pick phrase: **Ik meld een storing.**

**7 — Read aloud** your improved message.

**8 — Check:** is the request clear in one glance?
---

**All four skills — finish this lesson with these four micro-tasks**

🎧 **Listening:** Use TTS on this Dutch line (slow, then closer to natural): *Ik wil vandaag dokter. Zonder afspraak. Nu.*

📖 **Reading:** Read silently, then say the **main idea in one English sentence**: *Ik wil vandaag dokter. Zonder afspraak. Nu.*

✍️ **Writing:** Write **one new Dutch sentence** that uses **hoofdpijn** and fits *Perfectum (hebben/zijn + voltooid deelwoord)*.

🗣️ **Speaking:** Read your new sentence **aloud twice** — exaggerate stress the first time, normal the second.

*(If this lesson was already heavy on one skill, treat that skill as “extra reps” — the point is you always touch all four.)*
steps.teacher_notes: Mix modalities; at least 6 tasks — all concrete above.

steps.activity:
**Phrase bank (use freely)**
• hoofdpijn
• keel
• neus
• verkouden
• apotheek
• recept
• paracetamol
• rust
• afspraak
• huisarts

**Your task:** Write or say **4–6 short sentences** in Dutch about this unit theme.
Rules: include **at least one question** and one connector (**en**, **maar**, or **want**).

**Example starter (you can change it):**
Vandaag … Ik wil … **want** … **En jij?**
steps.teacher_notes: Encourage intelligibility over perfection.

steps.activity:
**Quick self-check**

Try each question, then tap **Check answer**. The correct answer stays hidden until you submit.

**Tip:** Say Dutch options out loud if that helps.

**Afterwards:** If three or more felt easy, open **Quiz** for a short recap. If not, go back to **Guided practice** once more.
steps.teacher_notes: Rendered with interactive UI when interaction is present.

items.prompt: Pick the best reply when someone says **Hoe gaat het?**
items.feedbackCorrect: Nice — this is a natural, friendly reply.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Tot ziens** is goodbye; **Graag gedaan** fits after *dank je*. After *Hoe gaat het?* people often say how they feel, e.g. **Goed, dank je!**

items.prompt: Complete: **Ik ___ Nederlands sinds januari.**
items.hint: Present tense of *leren* (to learn), 1st person singular.
items.feedbackCorrect: Yes — *Ik leer Nederlands sinds januari.*
items.feedbackIncorrect: The present of *leren* (ik-form) is **leer**.

items.prompt: Which opening sounds **more formal** when you talk to a stranger or in a shop?
items.feedbackCorrect: Right — **u** + verb form (*Kunt u…*) is the usual polite pattern.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Kunt u** with *u* is more formal; **Kun je** is fine with friends.

items.prompt: In Dutch, yes/no questions often put the **verb before the subject** (e.g. *Ga je mee?*).
items.feedbackCorrect: Correct — verb-first order is typical for yes/no questions.
items.feedbackIncorrect: This is **true**: many yes/no questions start with the verb.

items.prompt: Say or write **one new sentence** using **hoofdpijn**.
items.exampleAnswer: Example: *Ik wil graag hoofdpijn met mijn nieuwe buren.* (adjust to your situation.)

steps.activity:
**Nice work.**

Next in this unit: **Culture & context · Unspoken rules you will bump into** — we keep building **Health & body** with a new focus.

**2-minute revision:** skim the phrase bank from step 6 once more.

When you're ready, open **Quiz** for a compact recap — or **Flashcards** for the lemmas.

**Dutch culture insight**

**Apotheek** staff explain *hoe vaak* and *met eten* patiently; repeating back dosage (*drie keer per dag*) shows you understood.

**Phrases — GP & pharmacy**

| Dutch | English |
|-------|--------|
| *Ik wil graag een afspraak maken.* | I'd like to make an appointment. |
| *Ik ben verkouden sinds maandag.* | I've had a cold since Monday. |
| *Hoe vaak moet ik deze pillen innemen?* | How often should I take these pills? |

**Mini dialogue**

**Assistent:** Huisartsenpraktijk Noord, goedemiddag.
**Patiënt:** Goedemiddag, ik heb last van mijn keel en lichte koorts.
**Assistent:** Dokter kan u om 16:10 zien. Neem uw zorgpas mee.

**Say or write:** Name one symptom + duration in Dutch.

*Optional reflection:* In one English sentence, what feels similar or different where you live?
steps.teacher_notes: Link to next lesson id in CMS when wired.

quiz_ideas: Self-check: choose the best reply to "Hoe gaat het?" (see step 7 in the lesson).
Gap-fill idea: write a sentence with **hoofdpijn** in the present tense.
True/false: in Dutch yes/no questions the verb often comes before the subject.
Say one new polite request starting with **Kunt u …** or **Mag ik …**.
Paraphrase one Dutch line from the lesson input in your own English.
assessment.success_criteria: You can complete the step-7 self-check comfortably and produce at least one clear Dutch sentence about the lesson theme (spoken or written).

steps.activity:
**Culture snapshot — the Netherlands**

The huisarts (GP) is the usual first contact; emergency line 112 is for life-threatening situations; pharmacies (apotheek) advise on dosage.

**Mini text (Dutch):**
In veel appartementen staat een huisregel: na 22:00 rust op de gang. Buren lossen kleine problemen vaak eerst met een kort gesprek.

*In many flats there is a rule: quiet in the corridor after 22:00. Neighbours often solve small issues with a short chat first.*
steps.teacher_notes: Optional slow audio for D/A.

steps.activity:
**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — stress:** stress content words: *Ik wil een afspraak maken* (not every syllable equal).

**Useful phrases for talking about customs**

• **In Nederland …** — In the Netherlands …
• **Bij ons …** — Where I'm from …
• **Mag ik vragen …?** — May I ask …?

**Context:** The huisarts (GP) is the usual first contact; emergency line 112 is for life-threatening situations; pharmacies (apotheek) advise on dosage.

**Say or write:** one respectful question you could ask a Dutch friend about a tradition.
steps.teacher_notes: Max 2–3 patterns at A2.

steps.activity:
**1 — Match:** Koningsdag — orange / Sinterklaas — December gifts / kerst — family meal (discuss).

**2 — Institution:** where do you register an address? *(hint: **gemeente**)*

**3 — Sort:** "Direct can still be polite" — **waar / niet waar**?

**4 — Gist:** what is **rust** on a building notice about?

**5 — Reflect:** one difference between your country and NL (English ok).

**6 — Gap-fill:** In Nederland fietsen veel mensen, _____ het is plat.

**7 — Scenario:** friend cancels last minute — what do many Dutch people appreciate?

**8 — Your question in Dutch:** **Mag ik vragen hoe …?** finish it.

**Culture reminder:** The huisarts (GP) is the usual first contact; emergency line 112 is for life-threatening situations; pharmacies (apotheek) advise on dosage.
---

**All four skills — finish this lesson with these four micro-tasks**

🎧 **Listening:** Use TTS on this Dutch line (slow, then closer to natural): *Ik voel me vandaag niet zo goed.*

📖 **Reading:** Read silently, then say the **main idea in one English sentence**: *Ik voel me vandaag niet zo goed.*

✍️ **Writing:** Write **one new Dutch sentence** that uses **verkouden** and fits *Reflexive verbs (zich voelen, zich wassen)*.

🗣️ **Speaking:** Read your new sentence **aloud twice** — exaggerate stress the first time, normal the second.

*(If this lesson was already heavy on one skill, treat that skill as “extra reps” — the point is you always touch all four.)*

steps.activity:
**Listen & read** this short conversation. Read twice: first for gist, then for details.

**Patiënt:** Mijn keel doet zeer als ik slik.

**Arts:** Open uw mond even… Het ziet er rood uit. Veel thee en rust.

**Patiënt:** Mag ik sporten?

**Arts:** Liever deze week licht. Als het beter voelt, mag het weer.

**Gist check:** Who are the people, and what is the situation?
**Detail check:** Note one time phrase or place you recognise.

steps.activity:
**1 — Match** (say the pairs out loud)
• **paracetamol** ↔ a word you connect with daily life
• **rust** ↔ a place or thing from this unit

**2 — Gap-fill**
Complete: Ik _____ om acht uur. *(opstaan → stem: Ik **sta** … **op**)*

**3 — Mini dialogue**
**A:** Hoe laat ben je klaar? **B:** Om half zes, denk ik.

**4 — Find the odd one**
Which word fits *least* with this unit: **afspraak**, **broodje**, **trein**? Why?

**5 — True / false**
Say **waar** or **niet waar**: "In Dutch, the verb often moves when you ask a yes/no question."

**6 — Your sentence**
Write one true sentence about yourself using **paracetamol** or **rust**.

**7 — Transform**
Make this informal line a bit more formal: "Kun je me helpen?" → start with **Kunt u …**

**8 — Quick translation check**
"Je wast je handen voor het eten." — what does it mean in English (your own words)?

items.prompt: Which opening sounds **more formal** when you talk to a stranger or in a shop?
items.feedbackCorrect: Right — **u** + verb form (*Kunt u…*) is the usual polite pattern.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Kunt u** with *u* is more formal; **Kun je** is fine with friends.

items.prompt: In Dutch, yes/no questions often put the **verb before the subject** (e.g. *Ga je mee?*).
items.feedbackCorrect: Correct — verb-first order is typical for yes/no questions.
items.feedbackIncorrect: This is **true**: many yes/no questions start with the verb.

items.prompt: Say or write **one new sentence** using **paracetamol**.
items.exampleAnswer: Example: *Ik wil graag paracetamol met mijn nieuwe buren.* (adjust to your situation.)

**Dutch culture insight**

Sport and **bewegen** are mainstream small talk. *Ik ga naar de fysio* or *Ik moet meer stappen* are easy ways to bond lightly.

| Dutch | English |
|-------|--------|

| *Hoe vaak moet ik deze pillen innemen?* | How often should I take these pills? |

**Mini dialogue**

**Assistent:** Huisartsenpraktijk Noord, goedemiddag.
**Patiënt:** Goedemiddag, ik heb last van mijn keel en lichte koorts.
**Assistent:** Dokter kan u om 16:10 zien. Neem uw zorgpas mee.

steps.activity:
Welcome! This lesson is built in small steps so you always know where you are.

**What you'll be able to do:** Understand short input about work & study, notice useful chunks, and answer gist/detail questions

**Grammar thread today:** Purpose with om … te — you'll get explanations, examples, and production tasks in **Language focus** and **Guided practice**.

Take your time — you can repeat any step. Try to say at least one Dutch sentence out loud today.
steps.teacher_notes: Keep under ~1 min; reassure anxious learners.

steps.activity:
Let's wake up vocabulary about **Work & study**.

Words you might use today:
• **collega**
• **vergadering**
• **deadline**
• **pauze**
• **dienst**
• **rooster**
• **stage**
• **opleiding**

**Your turn (1–2 short sentences, Dutch or mixed):**
**Wat deed je gisteren?** — *What did you do yesterday?*

If you only know a few words, that's fine — start there.
steps.teacher_notes: Accept partial Dutch; encourage gestures.
steps.example_response: Gisteren had ik een drukke dag op kantoor. Na het werk maakte ik aantekeningen voor een cursus en belde ik een collega kort.

steps.activity:
**Listen & read** this short conversation. Read twice: first for gist, then for details.

**Manager:** Kunnen we de deadline naar vrijdag verschuiven?

**Jij:** Ja, als ik hulp krijg bij het testen.

**Manager:** Dan vraag ik Lisa om mee te kijken. Stuur een korte update in Teams.

**Jij:** Prima. Ik stuur vandaag nog een concept.

**Gist check:** Who are the people, and what is the situation?
**Detail check:** Note one time phrase or place you recognise.
steps.teacher_notes: Optional slow audio for D/A.

steps.activity:
**Today's grammar — *om … te* (purpose)**

**Rule:** *Ik bel **om** een afspraak **te** maken.* Infinitive at the end.

**Patterns:**
• *We mailen om de tijd te bevestigen.* · *Ik kom om een vraag te stellen.*

**Produce:**
1. Why you call the gemeente or GP in one *om … te* sentence.
2. Second sentence with a different verb.

**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — final -n:** *eten, gaan* — don’t swallow the *-n* completely in careful speech.

**Language focus — patterns you can reuse**

**infinitive clauses with om … te for purpose**
• NL: Ik bel om een afspraak te maken. / We mailen om de deadline te bevestigen.
• EN: I'm calling to make an appointment. / We're emailing to confirm the deadline.

**separable verbs in work context (meeting inplannen, doorgeven)**
• NL: Ik heb het verslag gisteren afgerond. / Ben je op tijd bij de vergadering gekomen?
• EN: I finished the report yesterday. / Did you get to the meeting on time?

**present perfect recognition (ik heb … gedaan) for experience**
• NL: Goedemorgen, ik werk vandaag thuis. / Met vriendelijke groet, …
• EN: Good morning, I'm working from home today. / Kind regards, …

**Try this:** pick one Dutch sentence above and change only the time or the person (ik → wij / vandaag → morgen), **or** do the “Produce” task from the grammar box above.


**Culture tip:** Work emails often start with Goedemorgen/Goedemiddag + name; lunch breaks are commonly around 30 minutes; part-time work is frequent.
*How does this compare with where you live? One sentence in your own words.*
steps.teacher_notes: Max 2–3 patterns at A2.

steps.activity:
**1 — Match** (say the pairs out loud)
• **collega** ↔ a word you connect with daily life
• **vergadering** ↔ a place or thing from this unit

**2 — Gap-fill**
Complete: Ik _____ om acht uur. *(opstaan → stem: Ik **sta** … **op**)*

**3 — Mini dialogue**
**A:** Hoe laat ben je klaar? **B:** Om half zes, denk ik.

**4 — Find the odd one**
Which word fits *least* with this unit: **deadline**, **broodje**, **trein**? Why?

**5 — True / false**
Say **waar** or **niet waar**: "In Dutch, the verb often moves when you ask a yes/no question."

**6 — Your sentence**
Write one true sentence about yourself using **collega** or **vergadering**.

**7 — Transform**
Make this informal line a bit more formal: "Kun je me helpen?" → start with **Kunt u …**

**8 — Quick translation check**
"Ik bel om een afspraak te maken." — what does it mean in English (your own words)?
---

**All four skills — finish this lesson with these four micro-tasks**

🎧 **Listening:** Use TTS on this Dutch line (slow, then closer to natural): *Manager: Kunnen we de deadline naar vrijdag verschuiven?*

📖 **Reading:** Read silently, then say the **main idea in one English sentence**: *Manager: Kunnen we de deadline naar vrijdag verschuiven?*

✍️ **Writing:** Write **one new Dutch sentence** that uses **collega** and fits *Purpose with om … te*.

🗣️ **Speaking:** Read your new sentence **aloud twice** — exaggerate stress the first time, normal the second.

*(If this lesson was already heavy on one skill, treat that skill as “extra reps” — the point is you always touch all four.)*
steps.teacher_notes: Mix modalities; at least 6 tasks — all concrete above.

items.prompt: Pick the best reply when someone says **Hoe gaat het?**
items.feedbackCorrect: Nice — this is a natural, friendly reply.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Tot ziens** is goodbye; **Graag gedaan** fits after *dank je*. After *Hoe gaat het?* people often say how they feel, e.g. **Goed, dank je!**

items.prompt: Complete: **Ik ___ Nederlands sinds januari.**
items.hint: Present tense of *leren* (to learn), 1st person singular.
items.feedbackCorrect: Yes — *Ik leer Nederlands sinds januari.*
items.feedbackIncorrect: The present of *leren* (ik-form) is **leer**.

items.prompt: Which opening sounds **more formal** when you talk to a stranger or in a shop?
items.feedbackCorrect: Right — **u** + verb form (*Kunt u…*) is the usual polite pattern.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Kunt u** with *u* is more formal; **Kun je** is fine with friends.

items.prompt: In Dutch, yes/no questions often put the **verb before the subject** (e.g. *Ga je mee?*).
items.feedbackCorrect: Correct — verb-first order is typical for yes/no questions.
items.feedbackIncorrect: This is **true**: many yes/no questions start with the verb.

items.prompt: Say or write **one new sentence** using **collega**.
items.exampleAnswer: Example: *Ik wil graag collega met mijn nieuwe buren.* (adjust to your situation.)

steps.activity:
**Nice work.**

Next in this unit: **Listen & read · Names, places & plans in context** — we keep building **Work & study** with a new focus.

**2-minute revision:** skim the phrase bank from step 6 once more.

When you're ready, open **Quiz** for a compact recap — or **Flashcards** for the lemmas.

**Dutch culture insight**

Emails often open with **Goedemorgen** / **Goedemiddag** + the name; closings use *Met vriendelijke groet* or a shorter *Groet* among close colleagues. Subject lines stay concrete.

**Phrases — e-mail & office**

| Dutch | English |
|-------|--------|
| *Goedemorgen, ik werk vandaag thuis.* | Good morning, I'm WFH today. |
| *Kunt u het concept vandaag nog sturen?* | Could you send the draft today? |
| *Met vriendelijke groet,* | Kind regards, |

**Mini dialogue**

**A:** Kun je donderdag om 10:00 vergaderen?
**B:** Helaas niet; kan het om 14:00?
**A:** Prima, ik stuur een Teams-uitnodiging.

**Say or write:** Reschedule one meeting in one polite Dutch sentence.

*Optional reflection:* In one English sentence, what feels similar or different where you live?

**Collega:** Heb je zin om mee te lunchen?

**Jij:** Graag, maar ik heb om twaalf een call. Om half een?

**Collega:** Past. Dan pakken we iets bij de kantine.

steps.activity:
**Today's grammar — perfectum (short)**

**Rule:** *hebben/zijn + voltooid deelwoord* for completed experiences: *Ik **heb** gewerkt*, *Ik **ben** gekomen*.

**Patterns:**
• *Gisteren heb ik boodschappen gedaan.* · *Ben je op tijd gekomen?*

**Produce:**
1. One sentence: what you did yesterday (*gisteren heb ik …*).
2. One question with *heb je …* or *ben je …*.

**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — v / f:** *vandaag, vier* — light *v*, not English *w*.

**Language focus — patterns you can reuse**

**infinitive clauses with om … te for purpose**
• NL: We mailen om de deadline te bevestigen. / Ik bel om een afspraak te maken.
• EN: We're emailing to confirm the deadline. / I'm calling to make an appointment.

**separable verbs in work context (meeting inplannen, doorgeven)**
• NL: Ben je op tijd bij de vergadering gekomen? / Ik heb het verslag gisteren afgerond.
• EN: Did you get to the meeting on time? / I finished the report yesterday.

**present perfect recognition (ik heb … gedaan) for experience**
• NL: Met vriendelijke groet, … / Goedemorgen, ik werk vandaag thuis.
• EN: Kind regards, … / Good morning, I'm working from home today.

**Try this:** pick one Dutch sentence above and change only the time or the person (ik → wij / vandaag → morgen), **or** do the “Produce” task from the grammar box above.
steps.teacher_notes: Max 2–3 patterns at A2.

**separable verbs in work context (meeting inplannen, doorgeven)**
• NL: Ik heb het verslag gisteren afgerond. / Ben je op tijd bij de vergadering gekomen?
• EN: I finished the report yesterday. / Did you get to the meeting on time?

**present perfect recognition (ik heb … gedaan) for experience**
• NL: Goedemorgen, ik werk vandaag thuis. / Met vriendelijke groet, …
• EN: Good morning, I'm working from home today. / Kind regards, …

Read them aloud once, slowly.
steps.teacher_notes: Optional slow audio for D/A.

steps.activity:
**Today's grammar — *om … te* (purpose)**

**Rule:** *Ik bel **om** een afspraak **te** maken.* Infinitive at the end.

**Patterns:**
• *We mailen om de tijd te bevestigen.* · *Ik kom om een vraag te stellen.*

**Produce:**
1. Why you call the gemeente or GP in one *om … te* sentence.
2. Second sentence with a different verb.

**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — g / ch:** practise *goed — licht — dag — nacht* with TTS; back *g* vs softer *ch*.

**Language focus — patterns you can reuse**

**infinitive clauses with om … te for purpose**
• NL: Ik bel om een afspraak te maken. / We mailen om de deadline te bevestigen.
• EN: I'm calling to make an appointment. / We're emailing to confirm the deadline.

**separable verbs in work context (meeting inplannen, doorgeven)**
• NL: Ik heb het verslag gisteren afgerond. / Ben je op tijd bij de vergadering gekomen?
• EN: I finished the report yesterday. / Did you get to the meeting on time?

**present perfect recognition (ik heb … gedaan) for experience**
• NL: Goedemorgen, ik werk vandaag thuis. / Met vriendelijke groet, …
• EN: Good morning, I'm working from home today. / Kind regards, …

**Try this:** pick one Dutch sentence above and change only the time or the person (ik → wij / vandaag → morgen), **or** do the “Produce” task from the grammar box above.
steps.teacher_notes: Max 2–3 patterns at A2.

steps.activity:
**1 — Match** (say the pairs out loud)
• **stage** ↔ a word you connect with daily life
• **opleiding** ↔ a place or thing from this unit

**2 — Gap-fill**
Complete: Ik _____ om acht uur. *(opstaan → stem: Ik **sta** … **op**)*

**3 — Mini dialogue**
**A:** Hoe laat ben je klaar? **B:** Om half zes, denk ik.

**4 — Find the odd one**
Which word fits *least* with this unit: **werkgever**, **broodje**, **trein**? Why?

**5 — True / false**
Say **waar** or **niet waar**: "In Dutch, the verb often moves when you ask a yes/no question."

**6 — Your sentence**
Write one true sentence about yourself using **stage** or **opleiding**.

**7 — Transform**
Make this informal line a bit more formal: "Kun je me helpen?" → start with **Kunt u …**

**8 — Quick translation check**
"Ik bel om een afspraak te maken." — what does it mean in English (your own words)?
---

**All four skills — finish this lesson with these four micro-tasks**

🎧 **Listening:** Use TTS on this Dutch line (slow, then closer to natural): *Ik bel om een afspraak te maken.*

📖 **Reading:** Read silently, then say the **main idea in one English sentence**: *Ik bel om een afspraak te maken.*

✍️ **Writing:** Write **one new Dutch sentence** that uses **stage** and fits *Purpose with om … te*.

🗣️ **Speaking:** Read your new sentence **aloud twice** — exaggerate stress the first time, normal the second.

*(If this lesson was already heavy on one skill, treat that skill as “extra reps” — the point is you always touch all four.)*
steps.teacher_notes: Mix modalities; at least 6 tasks — all concrete above.

steps.activity:

items.prompt: Pick the best reply when someone says **Hoe gaat het?**
items.feedbackCorrect: Nice — this is a natural, friendly reply.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Tot ziens** is goodbye; **Graag gedaan** fits after *dank je*. After *Hoe gaat het?* people often say how they feel, e.g. **Goed, dank je!**

items.prompt: Complete: **Ik ___ Nederlands sinds januari.**
items.hint: Present tense of *leren* (to learn), 1st person singular.
items.feedbackCorrect: Yes — *Ik leer Nederlands sinds januari.*
items.feedbackIncorrect: The present of *leren* (ik-form) is **leer**.

items.prompt: Which opening sounds **more formal** when you talk to a stranger or in a shop?
items.feedbackCorrect: Right — **u** + verb form (*Kunt u…*) is the usual polite pattern.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Kunt u** with *u* is more formal; **Kun je** is fine with friends.

items.prompt: In Dutch, yes/no questions often put the **verb before the subject** (e.g. *Ga je mee?*).
items.feedbackCorrect: Correct — verb-first order is typical for yes/no questions.
items.feedbackIncorrect: This is **true**: many yes/no questions start with the verb.

items.prompt: Say or write **one new sentence** using **stage**.
items.exampleAnswer: Example: *Ik wil graag stage met mijn nieuwe buren.* (adjust to your situation.)

steps.activity:
**Nice work.**

Next in this unit: **Reading · Read threads & signs like a local** — we keep building **Work & study** with a new focus.

**2-minute revision:** skim the phrase bank from step 6 once more.

When you're ready, open **Quiz** for a compact recap — or **Flashcards** for the lemmas.

**Dutch culture insight**

**Part-time** work is common; *vast* vs *tijdelijk* contracts matter for benefits. Asking *Hoeveel uur per week?* is normal when comparing jobs.

**Phrases — e-mail & office**

| Dutch | English |
|-------|--------|
| *Goedemorgen, ik werk vandaag thuis.* | Good morning, I'm WFH today. |
| *Kunt u het concept vandaag nog sturen?* | Could you send the draft today? |
| *Met vriendelijke groet,* | Kind regards, |

**Mini dialogue**

**A:** Kun je donderdag om 10:00 vergaderen?
**B:** Helaas niet; kan het om 14:00?
**A:** Prima, ik stuur een Teams-uitnodiging.

**Say or write:** Reschedule one meeting in one polite Dutch sentence.

*Optional reflection:* In one English sentence, what feels similar or different where you live?

**Rule:** *Geachte mevrouw/meneer*, concrete subject, *Met vriendelijke groet*.

**Produce:**

**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — ui / eu / ij:** round lips for *huis — leeuw*; *mijn* is one smooth glide.

• EN: Kind regards, … / Good morning, I'm working from home today.

Welcome! This lesson is built in small steps so you always know where you are.

**What you'll be able to do:** Complete a small real-world task about work & study (fix a message, choose replies, short role-card)

**Grammar thread today:** Perfectum (hebben/zijn + voltooid deelwoord) — you'll get explanations, examples, and production tasks in **Language focus** and **Guided practice**.

Take your time — you can repeat any step. Try to say at least one Dutch sentence out loud today.
steps.teacher_notes: Keep under ~1 min; reassure anxious learners.

steps.activity:
Let's wake up vocabulary about **Work & study**.

Words you might use today:
• **pauze**
• **dienst**
• **rooster**
• **stage**
• **opleiding**
• **werkgever**
• **salaris**

**Your turn (1–2 short sentences, Dutch or mixed):**

If you only know a few words, that's fine — start there.

steps.example_response: Gisteren had ik een drukke dag op kantoor. Na het werk maakte ik aantekeningen voor een cursus en belde ik een collega kort.

steps.activity:
**Mini situation**

You get a short message that sounds a bit too direct in Dutch. Your job in the next steps is to choose a polite fix.

**Message (Dutch):**
Stuur dat bestand vandaag. Geen excuses. Groet.

**Send that file today. No excuses. Regards.**

We will upgrade the tone without changing the facts.
steps.teacher_notes: Optional slow audio for D/A.

steps.activity:
**Today's grammar — perfectum (short)**

**Rule:** *hebben/zijn + voltooid deelwoord* for completed experiences: *Ik **heb** gewerkt*, *Ik **ben** gekomen*.

**Patterns:**
• *Gisteren heb ik boodschappen gedaan.* · *Ben je op tijd gekomen?*

**Produce:**
1. One sentence: what you did yesterday (*gisteren heb ik …*).
2. One question with *heb je …* or *ben je …*.

**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — stress:** stress content words: *Ik wil een afspraak maken* (not every syllable equal).

**Language focus — patterns you can reuse**

**infinitive clauses with om … te for purpose**
• NL: We mailen om de deadline te bevestigen. / Ik bel om een afspraak te maken.
• EN: We're emailing to confirm the deadline. / I'm calling to make an appointment.

**separable verbs in work context (meeting inplannen, doorgeven)**
• NL: Ben je op tijd bij de vergadering gekomen? / Ik heb het verslag gisteren afgerond.
• EN: Did you get to the meeting on time? / I finished the report yesterday.

**present perfect recognition (ik heb … gedaan) for experience**
• NL: Met vriendelijke groet, … / Goedemorgen, ik werk vandaag thuis.
• EN: Kind regards, … / Good morning, I'm working from home today.

**Try this:** pick one Dutch sentence above and change only the time or the person (ik → wij / vandaag → morgen), **or** do the “Produce” task from the grammar box above.
steps.teacher_notes: Max 2–3 patterns at A2.

steps.activity:
**1 — Tone:** which line feels too blunt for a landlord?

**2 — Rewrite opening** with **Goedemiddag** + **met vriendelijke groet** at the end.

**3 — Add one sentence** with the reason: *want de verwarming uitvalt bij kou.*

**4 — Choose best closing:** A) Groetjes B) Met vriendelijke groet C) Doei

**5 — Word order:** fix: *Ik wil graag een afspraak morgen.*

**6 — Scenario match:** pick phrase: **Ik meld een storing.**

**7 — Read aloud** your improved message.

**8 — Check:** is the request clear in one glance?
---

**All four skills — finish this lesson with these four micro-tasks**

🎧 **Listening:** Use TTS on this Dutch line (slow, then closer to natural): *Stuur dat bestand vandaag. Geen excuses. Groet.*

📖 **Reading:** Read silently, then say the **main idea in one English sentence**: *Stuur dat bestand vandaag. Geen excuses. Groet.*

✍️ **Writing:** Write **one new Dutch sentence** that uses **pauze** and fits *Perfectum (hebben/zijn + voltooid deelwoord)*.

🗣️ **Speaking:** Read your new sentence **aloud twice** — exaggerate stress the first time, normal the second.

*(If this lesson was already heavy on one skill, treat that skill as “extra reps” — the point is you always touch all four.)*
steps.teacher_notes: Mix modalities; at least 6 tasks — all concrete above.

**Phrase bank (use freely)**

steps.activity:
**Quick self-check**

Try each question, then tap **Check answer**. The correct answer stays hidden until you submit.

**Tip:** Say Dutch options out loud if that helps.

**Afterwards:** If three or more felt easy, open **Quiz** for a short recap. If not, go back to **Guided practice** once more.

items.prompt: Pick the best reply when someone says **Hoe gaat het?**
items.feedbackCorrect: Nice — this is a natural, friendly reply.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Tot ziens** is goodbye; **Graag gedaan** fits after *dank je*. After *Hoe gaat het?* people often say how they feel, e.g. **Goed, dank je!**

items.prompt: Complete: **Ik ___ Nederlands sinds januari.**
items.hint: Present tense of *leren* (to learn), 1st person singular.
items.feedbackCorrect: Yes — *Ik leer Nederlands sinds januari.*
items.feedbackIncorrect: The present of *leren* (ik-form) is **leer**.

items.prompt: Which opening sounds **more formal** when you talk to a stranger or in a shop?
items.feedbackCorrect: Right — **u** + verb form (*Kunt u…*) is the usual polite pattern.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Kunt u** with *u* is more formal; **Kun je** is fine with friends.

items.prompt: In Dutch, yes/no questions often put the **verb before the subject** (e.g. *Ga je mee?*).
items.feedbackCorrect: Correct — verb-first order is typical for yes/no questions.
items.feedbackIncorrect: This is **true**: many yes/no questions start with the verb.

items.prompt: Say or write **one new sentence** using **pauze**.
items.exampleAnswer: Example: *Ik wil graag pauze met mijn nieuwe buren.* (adjust to your situation.)

steps.activity:
**Nice work.**

Next in this unit: **Culture & context · Culture, not trivia—why it matters** — we keep building **Work & study** with a new focus.

**2-minute revision:** skim the phrase bank from step 6 once more.

When you're ready, open **Quiz** for a compact recap — or **Flashcards** for the lemmas.

**Dutch culture insight**

**Lunch** is often 30 minutes; some teams eat together, others at desks. *Mag ik iets te eten halen?* is a polite way to duck out.

**Phrases — e-mail & office**

| Dutch | English |
|-------|--------|
| *Goedemorgen, ik werk vandaag thuis.* | Good morning, I'm WFH today. |
| *Kunt u het concept vandaag nog sturen?* | Could you send the draft today? |
| *Met vriendelijke groet,* | Kind regards, |

**Mini dialogue**

**A:** Kun je donderdag om 10:00 vergaderen?
**B:** Helaas niet; kan het om 14:00?
**A:** Prima, ik stuur een Teams-uitnodiging.

**Say or write:** Reschedule one meeting in one polite Dutch sentence.

*Optional reflection:* In one English sentence, what feels similar or different where you live?

steps.activity:
**Culture snapshot — the Netherlands**

Work emails often start with Goedemorgen/Goedemiddag + name; lunch breaks are commonly around 30 minutes; part-time work is frequent.

**Mini text (Dutch):**
In veel appartementen staat een huisregel: na 22:00 rust op de gang. Buren lossen kleine problemen vaak eerst met een kort gesprek.

*In many flats there is a rule: quiet in the corridor after 22:00. Neighbours often solve small issues with a short chat first.*
steps.teacher_notes: Optional slow audio for D/A.

steps.activity:
**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — rhythm:** short chunks: *van de — naar het — met een* — run them together softly.

**Useful phrases for talking about customs**

• **In Nederland …** — In the Netherlands …
• **Bij ons …** — Where I'm from …
• **Mag ik vragen …?** — May I ask …?

**Context:** Work emails often start with Goedemorgen/Goedemiddag + name; lunch breaks are commonly around 30 minutes; part-time work is frequent.

**Say or write:** one respectful question you could ask a Dutch friend about a tradition.
steps.teacher_notes: Max 2–3 patterns at A2.

steps.activity:
**1 — Match:** Koningsdag — orange / Sinterklaas — December gifts / kerst — family meal (discuss).

**2 — Institution:** where do you register an address? *(hint: **gemeente**)*

**3 — Sort:** "Direct can still be polite" — **waar / niet waar**?

**4 — Gist:** what is **rust** on a building notice about?

**5 — Reflect:** one difference between your country and NL (English ok).

**6 — Gap-fill:** In Nederland fietsen veel mensen, _____ het is plat.

**7 — Scenario:** friend cancels last minute — what do many Dutch people appreciate?

**8 — Your question in Dutch:** **Mag ik vragen hoe …?** finish it.

**Culture reminder:** Work emails often start with Goedemorgen/Goedemiddag + name; lunch breaks are commonly around 30 minutes; part-time work is frequent.
---

📖 **Reading:** Read silently, then say the **main idea in one English sentence**: *Ik bel om een afspraak te maken.*

✍️ **Writing:** Write **one new Dutch sentence** that uses **stage** and fits *Purpose with om … te*.

🗣️ **Speaking:** Read your new sentence **aloud twice** — exaggerate stress the first time, normal the second.

*(If this lesson was already heavy on one skill, treat that skill as “extra reps” — the point is you always touch all four.)*

items.feedbackIncorrect: **Tot ziens** is goodbye; **Graag gedaan** fits after *dank je*. After *Hoe gaat het?* people often say how they feel, e.g. **Goed, dank je!**

items.prompt: Complete: **Ik ___ Nederlands sinds januari.**
items.hint: Present tense of *leren* (to learn), 1st person singular.
items.feedbackCorrect: Yes — *Ik leer Nederlands sinds januari.*
items.feedbackIncorrect: The present of *leren* (ik-form) is **leer**.

items.prompt: Which opening sounds **more formal** when you talk to a stranger or in a shop?
items.feedbackCorrect: Right — **u** + verb form (*Kunt u…*) is the usual polite pattern.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Kunt u** with *u* is more formal; **Kun je** is fine with friends.

items.prompt: In Dutch, yes/no questions often put the **verb before the subject** (e.g. *Ga je mee?*).
items.feedbackCorrect: Correct — verb-first order is typical for yes/no questions.
items.feedbackIncorrect: This is **true**: many yes/no questions start with the verb.

items.prompt: Say or write **one new sentence** using **salaris**.
items.exampleAnswer: Example: *Ik wil graag salaris met mijn nieuwe buren.* (adjust to your situation.)

steps.activity:
**Nice work.**

Next unit starts with **Listen & read · Names, places & plans in context**. You can preview the new words in Browse whenever you like.

**2-minute revision:** skim the phrase bank from step 6 once more.

When you're ready, open **Quiz** for a compact recap — or **Flashcards** for the lemmas.

**Dutch culture insight**

**Hybrid** patterns (*thuiswerken*, *op kantoor*) show up in planning talk. *Laten we een Teams-link delen* reflects everyday office Dutch.

**Phrases — e-mail & office**

| Dutch | English |
|-------|--------|
| *Goedemorgen, ik werk vandaag thuis.* | Good morning, I'm WFH today. |
| *Kunt u het concept vandaag nog sturen?* | Could you send the draft today? |
| *Met vriendelijke groet,* | Kind regards, |

**Mini dialogue**

**A:** Kun je donderdag om 10:00 vergaderen?
**B:** Helaas niet; kan het om 14:00?
**A:** Prima, ik stuur een Teams-uitnodiging.

**Say or write:** Reschedule one meeting in one polite Dutch sentence.

*Optional reflection:* In one English sentence, what feels similar or different where you live?
steps.teacher_notes: Link to next lesson id in CMS when wired.

steps.activity:
Welcome! This lesson is built in small steps so you always know where you are.

**What you'll be able to do:** Understand short input about admin & services, notice useful chunks, and answer gist/detail questions

**Grammar thread today:** Polite requests with zou … willen/kunnen — you'll get explanations, examples, and production tasks in **Language focus** and **Guided practice**.

Take your time — you can repeat any step. Try to say at least one Dutch sentence out loud today.
steps.teacher_notes: Keep under ~1 min; reassure anxious learners.

steps.activity:
Let's wake up vocabulary about **Admin & services**.

Words you might use today:
• **gemeente**
• **paspoort**
• **verblijfsvergunning**
• **DigiD**
• **afspraak**
• **loket**
• **wachtrij**
• **bankpas**

**Your turn (1–2 short sentences, Dutch or mixed):**
**Wat deed je gisteren?** — *What did you do yesterday?*

If you only know a few words, that's fine — start there.
steps.teacher_notes: Accept partial Dutch; encourage gestures.
steps.example_response: Gisteren ging ik naar de **gemeente** voor een afspraak. Daarna deed ik een kopie van een document en postte ik een brief.

steps.activity:
**Listen & read** this short conversation. Read twice: first for gist, then for details.

**Balie:** Goedemorgen. U wilt inschrijven?

**Inwoner:** Ja, ik ben verhuisd. Wat heb ik nodig?

**Balie:** Identiteitsbewijs en een adresbewijs van de laatste drie maanden.

**Inwoner:** Kan ik een afspraak online maken?

**Balie:** Ja, met DigiD op onze website. Anders help ik u hier.

**Gist check:** Who are the people, and what is the situation?
**Detail check:** Note one time phrase or place you recognise.
steps.teacher_notes: Optional slow audio for D/A.

steps.activity:
**Today's grammar — softening with *zou***

**Rule:** *Ik zou graag … willen/kunnen* sounds careful at desks and on the phone.

**Patterns:**
• *Ik zou graag een afspraak willen.* · *Zou u dat kunnen herhalen?*

**Produce:**
1. One formal request with *Ik zou graag …*.
2. One follow-up: *Zou u … kunnen …?*

**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — v / f:** *vandaag, vier* — light *v*, not English *w*.

**Language focus — patterns you can reuse**

**conditional polite forms (Ik zou graag … willen)**
• NL: Ik zou graag een afspraak willen voor een ID-kaart. / Zou u dit formulier kunnen controleren?
• EN: I'd like an appointment for an ID card. / Could you check this form?

**passive-like bureaucratic phrases (wordt verwerkt) — recognition**
• NL: Het document dat u nodig heeft, staat online. / De pasfoto die ik meeneem, is recent.
• EN: The document you need is online. / The passport photo I'm bringing is recent.

**relative clauses with die/dat (very short)**
• NL: Ik moet mijn adres bij de gemeente wijzigen. / Zonder DigiD loop ik naar het loket.
• EN: I have to change my address at the municipality. / Without DigiD I go to the desk.

**Try this:** pick one Dutch sentence above and change only the time or the person (ik → wij / vandaag → morgen), **or** do the “Produce” task from the grammar box above.


**Culture tip:** Many gemeenten use DigiD for online services; without DigiD you often need to visit in person with ID and proof of address.
*How does this compare with where you live? One sentence in your own words.*
steps.teacher_notes: Max 2–3 patterns at A2.

steps.activity:
**1 — Match** (say the pairs out loud)
• **gemeente** ↔ a word you connect with daily life
• **paspoort** ↔ a place or thing from this unit

**2 — Gap-fill**
Complete: Ik _____ om acht uur. *(opstaan → stem: Ik **sta** … **op**)*

**3 — Mini dialogue**
**A:** Hoe laat ben je klaar? **B:** Om half zes, denk ik.

**4 — Find the odd one**
Which word fits *least* with this unit: **verblijfsvergunning**, **broodje**, **trein**? Why?

**5 — True / false**
Say **waar** or **niet waar**: "In Dutch, the verb often moves when you ask a yes/no question."

**6 — Your sentence**
Write one true sentence about yourself using **gemeente** or **paspoort**.

**7 — Transform**
Make this informal line a bit more formal: "Kun je me helpen?" → start with **Kunt u …**

**8 — Quick translation check**
"Ik zou graag een afspraak willen voor een ID-kaart." — what does it mean in English (your own words)?
---

**All four skills — finish this lesson with these four micro-tasks**

🎧 **Listening:** Use TTS on this Dutch line (slow, then closer to natural): *Balie: Goedemorgen. U wilt inschrijven?*

📖 **Reading:** Read silently, then say the **main idea in one English sentence**: *Balie: Goedemorgen. U wilt inschrijven?*

✍️ **Writing:** Write **one new Dutch sentence** that uses **gemeente** and fits *Polite requests with zou … willen/kunnen*.

🗣️ **Speaking:** Read your new sentence **aloud twice** — exaggerate stress the first time, normal the second.

*(If this lesson was already heavy on one skill, treat that skill as “extra reps” — the point is you always touch all four.)*
steps.teacher_notes: Mix modalities; at least 6 tasks — all concrete above.

steps.activity:
**Nice work.**

Next in this unit: **Listen & read · Conversation routines you can reuse** — we keep building **Admin & services** with a new focus.

**2-minute revision:** skim the phrase bank from step 6 once more.

When you're ready, open **Quiz** for a compact recap — or **Flashcards** for the lemmas.

**Dutch culture insight**

**DigiD** unlocks many gemeente tasks online; without it you queue with ID and proof of address. *Afspraak maken* beats walking in cold in many cities.

**Phrases — gemeente desk**

| Dutch | English |
|-------|--------|
| *Ik zou graag een afspraak willen.* | I'd like an appointment. |
| *Welke documenten heb ik nodig?* | Which documents do I need? |
| *Kan ik dit online met DigiD?* | Can I do this online with DigiD? |

**Mini dialogue**

**Balie:** Goedemorgen, waarmee kan ik u helpen?
**Inwoner:** Ik wil me inschrijven op dit adres. Wat moet ik laten zien?
**Balie:** Paspoort, huurcontract en een bankafschrift van de afgelopen maand.

**Say or write:** Ask what you need to bring for one appointment.

*Optional reflection:* In one English sentence, what feels similar or different where you live?

**Klant:** Ik heb een nieuwe pinpas nodig. De oude is kapot.

**Bankmedewerker:** Geen probleem. Identiteitsbewijs, alstublieft. De nieuwe pas komt binnen vijf dagen.

**Klant:** Moet ik mijn rekeningnummer weten?

steps.activity:
**Today's grammar — *die* / *dat* (short relatives)**

**Rule:** *Het formulier **dat** u nodig heeft* — *de pasfoto **die** u meeneemt*.

**Patterns:**
• *Het document dat online staat.* · *De bon die ik zoek.*

**Produce:**
1. Combine two short ideas with one *die/dat* clause.
2. Read aloud slowly — stress the noun, not every function word.

**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — g / ch:** practise *goed — licht — dag — nacht* with TTS; back *g* vs softer *ch*.

**Language focus — patterns you can reuse**

**conditional polite forms (Ik zou graag … willen)**
• NL: Zou u dit formulier kunnen controleren? / Ik zou graag een afspraak willen voor een ID-kaart.
• EN: Could you check this form? / I'd like an appointment for an ID card.

**passive-like bureaucratic phrases (wordt verwerkt) — recognition**
• NL: De pasfoto die ik meeneem, is recent. / Het document dat u nodig heeft, staat online.
• EN: The passport photo I'm bringing is recent. / The document you need is online.

**relative clauses with die/dat (very short)**
• NL: Zonder DigiD loop ik naar het loket. / Ik moet mijn adres bij de gemeente wijzigen.
• EN: Without DigiD I go to the desk. / I have to change my address at the municipality.

**Try this:** pick one Dutch sentence above and change only the time or the person (ik → wij / vandaag → morgen), **or** do the “Produce” task from the grammar box above.

steps.activity:
**Input — patterns**

Below are sentences from this theme. They show word order you can copy.

**conditional polite forms (Ik zou graag … willen)**
• NL: Ik zou graag een afspraak willen voor een ID-kaart. / Zou u dit formulier kunnen controleren?
• EN: I'd like an appointment for an ID card. / Could you check this form?

**passive-like bureaucratic phrases (wordt verwerkt) — recognition**
• NL: Het document dat u nodig heeft, staat online. / De pasfoto die ik meeneem, is recent.
• EN: The document you need is online. / The passport photo I'm bringing is recent.

**relative clauses with die/dat (very short)**
• NL: Ik moet mijn adres bij de gemeente wijzigen. / Zonder DigiD loop ik naar het loket.
• EN: I have to change my address at the municipality. / Without DigiD I go to the desk.

Read them aloud once, slowly.

steps.activity:
**Today's grammar — softening with *zou***

**Rule:** *Ik zou graag … willen/kunnen* sounds careful at desks and on the phone.

**Patterns:**
• *Ik zou graag een afspraak willen.* · *Zou u dat kunnen herhalen?*

**Produce:**
1. One formal request with *Ik zou graag …*.
2. One follow-up: *Zou u … kunnen …?*

**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — ui / eu / ij:** round lips for *huis — leeuw*; *mijn* is one smooth glide.

**Language focus — patterns you can reuse**

**conditional polite forms (Ik zou graag … willen)**
• NL: Ik zou graag een afspraak willen voor een ID-kaart. / Zou u dit formulier kunnen controleren?
• EN: I'd like an appointment for an ID card. / Could you check this form?

**passive-like bureaucratic phrases (wordt verwerkt) — recognition**
• NL: Het document dat u nodig heeft, staat online. / De pasfoto die ik meeneem, is recent.
• EN: The document you need is online. / The passport photo I'm bringing is recent.

**relative clauses with die/dat (very short)**
• NL: Ik moet mijn adres bij de gemeente wijzigen. / Zonder DigiD loop ik naar het loket.
• EN: I have to change my address at the municipality. / Without DigiD I go to the desk.

**Try this:** pick one Dutch sentence above and change only the time or the person (ik → wij / vandaag → morgen), **or** do the “Produce” task from the grammar box above.
steps.teacher_notes: Max 2–3 patterns at A2.

steps.activity:
**1 — Match** (say the pairs out loud)
• **wachtrij** ↔ a word you connect with daily life
• **bankpas** ↔ a place or thing from this unit

**2 — Gap-fill**
Complete: Ik _____ om acht uur. *(opstaan → stem: Ik **sta** … **op**)*

**3 — Mini dialogue**
**A:** Hoe laat ben je klaar? **B:** Om half zes, denk ik.

**4 — Find the odd one**
Which word fits *least* with this unit: **rekening**, **broodje**, **trein**? Why?

**5 — True / false**
Say **waar** or **niet waar**: "In Dutch, the verb often moves when you ask a yes/no question."

**6 — Your sentence**
Write one true sentence about yourself using **wachtrij** or **bankpas**.

**7 — Transform**
Make this informal line a bit more formal: "Kun je me helpen?" → start with **Kunt u …**

**8 — Quick translation check**
"Ik zou graag een afspraak willen voor een ID-kaart." — what does it mean in English (your own words)?
---

**All four skills — finish this lesson with these four micro-tasks**

🎧 **Listening:** Use TTS on this Dutch line (slow, then closer to natural): *Ik zou graag een afspraak willen voor een ID-kaart.*

📖 **Reading:** Read silently, then say the **main idea in one English sentence**: *Ik zou graag een afspraak willen voor een ID-kaart.*

✍️ **Writing:** Write **one new Dutch sentence** that uses **wachtrij** and fits *Polite requests with zou … willen/kunnen*.

🗣️ **Speaking:** Read your new sentence **aloud twice** — exaggerate stress the first time, normal the second.

*(If this lesson was already heavy on one skill, treat that skill as “extra reps” — the point is you always touch all four.)*
steps.teacher_notes: Mix modalities; at least 6 tasks — all concrete above.

items.prompt: Pick the best reply when someone says **Hoe gaat het?**
items.feedbackCorrect: Nice — this is a natural, friendly reply.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Tot ziens** is goodbye; **Graag gedaan** fits after *dank je*. After *Hoe gaat het?* people often say how they feel, e.g. **Goed, dank je!**

items.prompt: Complete: **Ik ___ Nederlands sinds januari.**
items.hint: Present tense of *leren* (to learn), 1st person singular.
items.feedbackCorrect: Yes — *Ik leer Nederlands sinds januari.*
items.feedbackIncorrect: The present of *leren* (ik-form) is **leer**.

items.prompt: Which opening sounds **more formal** when you talk to a stranger or in a shop?
items.feedbackCorrect: Right — **u** + verb form (*Kunt u…*) is the usual polite pattern.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Kunt u** with *u* is more formal; **Kun je** is fine with friends.

items.prompt: In Dutch, yes/no questions often put the **verb before the subject** (e.g. *Ga je mee?*).
items.feedbackCorrect: Correct — verb-first order is typical for yes/no questions.
items.feedbackIncorrect: This is **true**: many yes/no questions start with the verb.

items.prompt: Say or write **one new sentence** using **wachtrij**.
items.exampleAnswer: Example: *Ik wil graag wachtrij met mijn nieuwe buren.* (adjust to your situation.)

steps.activity:

Next in this unit: **Listening · Slow it down; catch every clue** — we keep building **Admin & services** with a new focus.

**Dutch culture insight**

**Paspoort**, **ID**, **BSN**, and **verblijfsvergunning** are vocabulary you hear in real queues. Photocopies (*kopie*) are sometimes needed even when you have scans.

**Phrases — gemeente desk**

| Dutch | English |
|-------|--------|
| *Ik zou graag een afspraak willen.* | I'd like an appointment. |
| *Welke documenten heb ik nodig?* | Which documents do I need? |
| *Kan ik dit online met DigiD?* | Can I do this online with DigiD? |

**Mini dialogue**

**Balie:** Goedemorgen, waarmee kan ik u helpen?
**Inwoner:** Ik wil me inschrijven op dit adres. Wat moet ik laten zien?
**Balie:** Paspoort, huurcontract en een bankafschrift van de afgelopen maand.

steps.activity:
**Listening focus**

Play the audio twice if you can (slow speed first is fine).

**Transcript** (read along on the second listen):

Balie: Gemeente Utrecht, waarmee kan ik u helpen?
Inwoner: Ik wil een afspraak voor een ID-kaart. Wat moet ik meenemen?
Balie: Pasfoto, uw oude ID of geboorteakte, en bewijs van adres.
Inwoner: Kan ik online een tijd kiezen?
Balie: Ja, via de site met DigiD. Anders kan ik hier een nummer voor u printen.

**While you listen:** underline in your mind who wants what and any numbers you hear.

---

**Script — gemeente desk**

*Inwoner:* Goedemorgen, ik zou graag een afspraak willen voor een ID-kaart.
*Balie:* Zeker. Heeft u DigiD? Zo niet, dan maak ik hier een nummer voor u.

**Listen task:** what does the clerk ask first?

**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**

**relative clauses with die/dat (very short)**
• NL: Zonder DigiD loop ik naar het loket. / Ik moet mijn adres bij de gemeente wijzigen.
• EN: Without DigiD I go to the desk. / I have to change my address at the municipality.

**Try this:** pick one Dutch sentence above and change only the time or the person (ik → wij / vandaag → morgen), **or** do the “Produce” task from the grammar box above.

steps.activity:
**Model message (Dutch)**

Subject: Vraag over bon

Goedemiddag,

Gisteren heb ik boodschappen gedaan bij uw winkel. Ik mis de bon in mijn tas. Kunt u een kopie mailen?

Met vriendelijke groet,
[jouw naam]

**Notice:** polite opening, short problem, clear request, closing.

**Later you will write:** Schrijf een formele zin + 3 korte punten voor de gemeente: je meldt een adreswijziging.
steps.teacher_notes: Optional slow audio for D/A.

steps.activity:
**Today's grammar — softening with *zou***

**Rule:** *Ik zou graag … willen/kunnen* sounds careful at desks and on the phone.

**Patterns:**
• *Ik zou graag een afspraak willen.* · *Zou u dat kunnen herhalen?*

**Produce:**
1. One formal request with *Ik zou graag …*.
2. One follow-up: *Zou u … kunnen …?*

**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — stress:** stress content words: *Ik wil een afspraak maken* (not every syllable equal).

**Pronunciation spotlight — sentence stress**

Say: **Ik wil een AFspraak bij de gemeenTE.** — stress the content words, relax the small ones.
Repeat twice: first exaggerated, then natural.



**Language focus — patterns you can reuse**

**conditional polite forms (Ik zou graag … willen)**
• NL: Ik zou graag een afspraak willen voor een ID-kaart. / Zou u dit formulier kunnen controleren?
• EN: I'd like an appointment for an ID card. / Could you check this form?

**passive-like bureaucratic phrases (wordt verwerkt) — recognition**
• NL: Het document dat u nodig heeft, staat online. / De pasfoto die ik meeneem, is recent.
• EN: The document you need is online. / The passport photo I'm bringing is recent.

**relative clauses with die/dat (very short)**
• NL: Ik moet mijn adres bij de gemeente wijzigen. / Zonder DigiD loop ik naar het loket.
• EN: I have to change my address at the municipality. / Without DigiD I go to the desk.

**Try this:** pick one Dutch sentence above and change only the time or the person (ik → wij / vandaag → morgen), **or** do the “Produce” task from the grammar box above.

steps.activity:
**1 — Label the parts:** opening / problem / request / closing in the model email.

**2 — Gap-fill:** Met vriendelijke ______, [naam].

**3 — Politeness:** choose softer Dutch: **Ik wil** → **Ik zou graag willen** in one sentence.

**4 — Combine:** Link with **want**: *Ik bel morgen, want ik ben vandaag druk.*

**5 — Shorten:** remove three words without losing the message.

**6 — Checklist tick:** subject line clear? closing present?

**7 — Your details:** replace [naam] and add one real detail from your life.

**8 — Read aloud:** read your draft slowly.
---

🎧 **Listening:** Use TTS on this Dutch line (slow, then closer to natural): *Ik zou graag een afspraak willen voor een ID-kaart.*

🗣️ **Speaking:** Read your new sentence **aloud twice** — exaggerate stress the first time, normal the second.

*(If this lesson was already heavy on one skill, treat that skill as “extra reps” — the point is you always touch all four.)*

items.prompt: Pick the best reply when someone says **Hoe gaat het?**
items.feedbackCorrect: Nice — this is a natural, friendly reply.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Tot ziens** is goodbye; **Graag gedaan** fits after *dank je*. After *Hoe gaat het?* people often say how they feel, e.g. **Goed, dank je!**

items.prompt: Complete: **Ik ___ Nederlands sinds januari.**
items.hint: Present tense of *leren* (to learn), 1st person singular.
items.feedbackCorrect: Yes — *Ik leer Nederlands sinds januari.*
items.feedbackIncorrect: The present of *leren* (ik-form) is **leer**.

items.prompt: Which opening sounds **more formal** when you talk to a stranger or in a shop?
items.feedbackCorrect: Right — **u** + verb form (*Kunt u…*) is the usual polite pattern.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Kunt u** with *u* is more formal; **Kun je** is fine with friends.

items.prompt: In Dutch, yes/no questions often put the **verb before the subject** (e.g. *Ga je mee?*).
items.feedbackCorrect: Correct — verb-first order is typical for yes/no questions.
items.feedbackIncorrect: This is **true**: many yes/no questions start with the verb.

steps.activity:
**Mini situation**

You get a short message that sounds a bit too direct in Dutch. Your job in the next steps is to choose a polite fix.

**Message (Dutch):**
Gemeente, ik heb geen zin in wachten. Geef meteen een paspoort.

**Municipality, I don't feel like waiting. Give a passport immediately.**

We will upgrade the tone without changing the facts.
steps.teacher_notes: Optional slow audio for D/A.

steps.activity:
**Today's grammar — formal e-mail frame**

**Rule:** *Geachte mevrouw/meneer*, concrete subject, *Met vriendelijke groet*.

**Patterns:**
• *Geachte mevrouw Jansen,* … *Met vriendelijke groet,*

**Produce:**
1. Write opening + one-line purpose + closing (placeholders ok).
2. Replace *Hoi* with a formal opener in a given line.

**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — rhythm:** short chunks: *van de — naar het — met een* — run them together softly.

**Language focus — patterns you can reuse**

**conditional polite forms (Ik zou graag … willen)**
• NL: Zou u dit formulier kunnen controleren? / Ik zou graag een afspraak willen voor een ID-kaart.
• EN: Could you check this form? / I'd like an appointment for an ID card.

**passive-like bureaucratic phrases (wordt verwerkt) — recognition**
• NL: De pasfoto die ik meeneem, is recent. / Het document dat u nodig heeft, staat online.
• EN: The passport photo I'm bringing is recent. / The document you need is online.

**relative clauses with die/dat (very short)**
• NL: Zonder DigiD loop ik naar het loket. / Ik moet mijn adres bij de gemeente wijzigen.
• EN: Without DigiD I go to the desk. / I have to change my address at the municipality.

**Try this:** pick one Dutch sentence above and change only the time or the person (ik → wij / vandaag → morgen), **or** do the “Produce” task from the grammar box above.
steps.teacher_notes: Max 2–3 patterns at A2.

steps.activity:
**1 — Tone:** which line feels too blunt for a landlord?

**2 — Rewrite opening** with **Goedemiddag** + **met vriendelijke groet** at the end.

**3 — Add one sentence** with the reason: *want de verwarming uitvalt bij kou.*

**4 — Choose best closing:** A) Groetjes B) Met vriendelijke groet C) Doei

**5 — Word order:** fix: *Ik wil graag een afspraak morgen.*

**6 — Scenario match:** pick phrase: **Ik meld een storing.**

**7 — Read aloud** your improved message.

**8 — Check:** is the request clear in one glance?
---

✍️ **Writing:** Write **one new Dutch sentence** that uses **DigiD** and fits *Formal e-mail openers and closings*.

🗣️ **Speaking:** Read your new sentence **aloud twice** — exaggerate stress the first time, normal the second.

*(If this lesson was already heavy on one skill, treat that skill as “extra reps” — the point is you always touch all four.)*

steps.activity:
**Culture snapshot — the Netherlands**

Many gemeenten use DigiD for online services; without DigiD you often need to visit in person with ID and proof of address.

**Mini text (Dutch):**
In veel appartementen staat een huisregel: na 22:00 rust op de gang. Buren lossen kleine problemen vaak eerst met een kort gesprek.

*In many flats there is a rule: quiet in the corridor after 22:00. Neighbours often solve small issues with a short chat first.*
steps.teacher_notes: Optional slow audio for D/A.

steps.activity:
**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — final -n:** *eten, gaan* — don’t swallow the *-n* completely in careful speech.

**Useful phrases for talking about customs**

• **In Nederland …** — In the Netherlands …
• **Bij ons …** — Where I'm from …
• **Mag ik vragen …?** — May I ask …?

**Context:** Many gemeenten use DigiD for online services; without DigiD you often need to visit in person with ID and proof of address.

**Say or write:** one respectful question you could ask a Dutch friend about a tradition.
steps.teacher_notes: Max 2–3 patterns at A2.

steps.activity:
**1 — Match:** Koningsdag — orange / Sinterklaas — December gifts / kerst — family meal (discuss).

**2 — Institution:** where do you register an address? *(hint: **gemeente**)*

**3 — Sort:** "Direct can still be polite" — **waar / niet waar**?

**4 — Gist:** what is **rust** on a building notice about?

**5 — Reflect:** one difference between your country and NL (English ok).

**6 — Gap-fill:** In Nederland fietsen veel mensen, _____ het is plat.

**7 — Scenario:** friend cancels last minute — what do many Dutch people appreciate?

**8 — Your question in Dutch:** **Mag ik vragen hoe …?** finish it.

**Culture reminder:** Many gemeenten use DigiD for online services; without DigiD you often need to visit in person with ID and proof of address.

📖 **Reading:** Read silently, then say the **main idea in one English sentence**: *Ik zou graag een afspraak willen voor een ID-kaart.*

✍️ **Writing:** Write **one new Dutch sentence** that uses **wachtrij** and fits *Polite requests with zou … willen/kunnen*.

🗣️ **Speaking:** Read your new sentence **aloud twice** — exaggerate stress the first time, normal the second.

*(If this lesson was already heavy on one skill, treat that skill as “extra reps” — the point is you always touch all four.)*

steps.activity:
**Listen & read** this short conversation. Read twice: first for gist, then for details.

**Postmedewerker:** Waar gaat dit pakket naartoe?

**Klant:** Naar Spanje. Hoeveel kost verzending?

**Postmedewerker:** Met track & trace is het veertien euro. Zonder trace goedkoper, maar langzamer.

steps.activity:
**Today's grammar — *die* / *dat* (short relatives)**

**Rule:** *Het formulier **dat** u nodig heeft* — *de pasfoto **die** u meeneemt*.

**Patterns:**
• *Het document dat online staat.* · *De bon die ik zoek.*

**Produce:**
1. Combine two short ideas with one *die/dat* clause.
2. Read aloud slowly — stress the noun, not every function word.

**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — v / f:** *vandaag, vier* — light *v*, not English *w*.

**Language focus — patterns you can reuse**

**conditional polite forms (Ik zou graag … willen)**
• NL: Zou u dit formulier kunnen controleren? / Ik zou graag een afspraak willen voor een ID-kaart.
• EN: Could you check this form? / I'd like an appointment for an ID card.

**passive-like bureaucratic phrases (wordt verwerkt) — recognition**
• NL: De pasfoto die ik meeneem, is recent. / Het document dat u nodig heeft, staat online.
• EN: The passport photo I'm bringing is recent. / The document you need is online.

**relative clauses with die/dat (very short)**
• NL: Zonder DigiD loop ik naar het loket. / Ik moet mijn adres bij de gemeente wijzigen.
• EN: Without DigiD I go to the desk. / I have to change my address at the municipality.

**Try this:** pick one Dutch sentence above and change only the time or the person (ik → wij / vandaag → morgen), **or** do the “Produce” task from the grammar box above.
steps.teacher_notes: Max 2–3 patterns at A2.

steps.activity:
**1 — Match** (say the pairs out loud)

Which word fits *least* with this unit: **handtekening**, **broodje**, **trein**? Why?

✍️ **Writing:** Write **one new Dutch sentence** that uses **overschrijving** and fits *Short relative clauses (die / dat)*.

items.prompt: Pick the best reply when someone says **Hoe gaat het?**
items.feedbackCorrect: Nice — this is a natural, friendly reply.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Tot ziens** is goodbye; **Graag gedaan** fits after *dank je*. After *Hoe gaat het?* people often say how they feel, e.g. **Goed, dank je!**

items.prompt: Complete: **Ik ___ Nederlands sinds januari.**
items.hint: Present tense of *leren* (to learn), 1st person singular.
items.feedbackCorrect: Yes — *Ik leer Nederlands sinds januari.*
items.feedbackIncorrect: The present of *leren* (ik-form) is **leer**.

items.prompt: Which opening sounds **more formal** when you talk to a stranger or in a shop?
items.feedbackCorrect: Right — **u** + verb form (*Kunt u…*) is the usual polite pattern.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Kunt u** with *u* is more formal; **Kun je** is fine with friends.

items.prompt: In Dutch, yes/no questions often put the **verb before the subject** (e.g. *Ga je mee?*).
items.feedbackCorrect: Correct — verb-first order is typical for yes/no questions.
items.feedbackIncorrect: This is **true**: many yes/no questions start with the verb.

steps.activity:
Let's wake up vocabulary about **Social & leisure**.

Words you might use today:
• **uitnodigen**
• **afzeggen**
• **sporten**
• **koor**
• **museum**
• **bioscoop**
• **festival**
• **vriendengroep**

**Your turn (1–2 short sentences, Dutch or mixed):**
**Wat deed je gisteren?** — *What did you do yesterday?*

If you only know a few words, that's fine — start there.
steps.teacher_notes: Accept partial Dutch; encourage gestures.
steps.example_response: Gisteren keek ik een serie en las ik een artikel online. Later belde ik mijn zus en we spraken over het weekend.

steps.activity:
**Listen & read** this short conversation. Read twice: first for gist, then for details.

**Vriend:** Zin om zondag naar het museum te gaan?

**Jij:** Ja! Welk tijdstip?

**Vriend:** Om elf uur, dan is het rustig. Daarna koffie?

**Jij:** Perfect. Ik koop de tickets online.

**Gist check:** Who are the people, and what is the situation?
**Detail check:** Note one time phrase or place you recognise.
steps.teacher_notes: Optional slow audio for D/A.

steps.activity:
**Today's grammar — *want* vs *omdat***

**Rule:** *want* keeps main word order in the second clause; *omdat* pushes the verb to the end (*omdat ik moe **ben***).

**Patterns:**
• *Ik blijf thuis, want ik ben ziek.* · *Ik blijf thuis, omdat ik ziek ben.*

**Produce:**
1. Same idea twice: once with *want*, once with *omdat* (keep it short).
2. Say which feels easier for you to hear in fast speech.

**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — g / ch:** practise *goed — licht — dag — nacht* with TTS; back *g* vs softer *ch*.

**Language focus — patterns you can reuse**

**want, omdat for reasons (simple clauses)**
• NL: Ik ga mee naar de film, want ik heb zin. / We blijven thuis, omdat het regent.
• EN: I'm coming to the film because I feel like it. / We're staying home because it's raining.

**graag, liever for preferences**
• NL: Ik ga liever met de fiets naar het station. / We drinken graag koffie op het terras.
• EN: I'd rather cycle to the station. / We like having coffee on the terrace.

**diminutives in informal speech (biertje, filmpje) — recognition/production**
• NL: Zullen we zaterdag om half zeven afspreken? / Ik nodig je uit voor een borrel.
• EN: Shall we meet Saturday at half past six? / I'm inviting you for drinks.

**Try this:** pick one Dutch sentence above and change only the time or the person (ik → wij / vandaag → morgen), **or** do the “Produce” task from the grammar box above.


**Culture tip:** Splitting the bill (iedereen betaalt voor zich) is common among friends; birthdays are often celebrated with coffee and cake at work.
*How does this compare with where you live? One sentence in your own words.*
steps.teacher_notes: Max 2–3 patterns at A2.

**Netflix**, **NPO**, and **podcasts** mix with *omroep* talk. Subtitles (*ondertiteling*) help learners; many Dutch people watch English shows with Dutch subs.

| Dutch | English |
|-------|--------|
| *Zin om zaterdag iets te drinken?* | Fancy a drink on Saturday? |

**B:** Liever niet vanavond, ik ben moe. Volgende week wel?
**A:** Top, dan plannen we woensdag.

**Rule:** *Ik ga graag naar …* · *Ik ga liever met de fiets* — soft opinions without fighting.

**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — ui / eu / ij:** round lips for *huis — leeuw*; *mijn* is one smooth glide.

• NL: Ik nodig je uit voor een borrel. / Zullen we zaterdag om half zeven afspreken?
• EN: I'm inviting you for drinks. / Shall we meet Saturday at half past six?

**Dutch culture insight**

**Verjaardagen** at work mean *taart* in the break room and a circle of handshakes or three kisses (region and generation vary). *Gefeliciteerd!* is your all-purpose word.

**Phrases — invites & Dutch directness**

| Dutch | English |
|-------|--------|
| *Zin om zaterdag iets te drinken?* | Fancy a drink on Saturday? |

steps.activity:
Welcome! This lesson is built in small steps so you always know where you are.

**What you'll be able to do:** Produce accurate sentence patterns about social & leisure using controlled substitution and drills

**Grammar thread today:** Reason clauses: want / omdat + word order — you'll get explanations, examples, and production tasks in **Language focus** and **Guided practice**.

steps.activity:
Let's wake up vocabulary about **Social & leisure**.

Words you might use today:
• **festival**
• **vriendengroep**
• **chatgroep**
• **voorstel**
• **afspraak**
• **gezellig**
• **uitnodigen**

steps.activity:
**Input — patterns**

Below are sentences from this theme. They show word order you can copy.

**want, omdat for reasons (simple clauses)**
• NL: Ik ga mee naar de film, want ik heb zin. / We blijven thuis, omdat het regent.
• EN: I'm coming to the film because I feel like it. / We're staying home because it's raining.

**graag, liever for preferences**
• NL: Ik ga liever met de fiets naar het station. / We drinken graag koffie op het terras.
• EN: I'd rather cycle to the station. / We like having coffee on the terrace.

**diminutives in informal speech (biertje, filmpje) — recognition/production**
• NL: Zullen we zaterdag om half zeven afspreken? / Ik nodig je uit voor een borrel.
• EN: Shall we meet Saturday at half past six? / I'm inviting you for drinks.

Read them aloud once, slowly.
steps.teacher_notes: Optional slow audio for D/A.

steps.activity:
**Today's grammar — *want* vs *omdat***

**Rule:** *want* keeps main word order in the second clause; *omdat* pushes the verb to the end (*omdat ik moe **ben***).

**Patterns:**
• *Ik blijf thuis, want ik ben ziek.* · *Ik blijf thuis, omdat ik ziek ben.*

**Produce:**
1. Same idea twice: once with *want*, once with *omdat* (keep it short).
2. Say which feels easier for you to hear in fast speech.

**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — sch / sj:** *school* vs *sjabloon* — listen twice, then shadow.

**Language focus — patterns you can reuse**

**want, omdat for reasons (simple clauses)**
• NL: Ik ga mee naar de film, want ik heb zin. / We blijven thuis, omdat het regent.
• EN: I'm coming to the film because I feel like it. / We're staying home because it's raining.

**graag, liever for preferences**
• NL: Ik ga liever met de fiets naar het station. / We drinken graag koffie op het terras.
• EN: I'd rather cycle to the station. / We like having coffee on the terrace.

**diminutives in informal speech (biertje, filmpje) — recognition/production**
• NL: Zullen we zaterdag om half zeven afspreken? / Ik nodig je uit voor een borrel.
• EN: Shall we meet Saturday at half past six? / I'm inviting you for drinks.

**Try this:** pick one Dutch sentence above and change only the time or the person (ik → wij / vandaag → morgen), **or** do the “Produce” task from the grammar box above.
steps.teacher_notes: Max 2–3 patterns at A2.

steps.activity:
**1 — Match** (say the pairs out loud)
• **festival** ↔ a word you connect with daily life
• **vriendengroep** ↔ a place or thing from this unit

**2 — Gap-fill**
Complete: Ik _____ om acht uur. *(opstaan → stem: Ik **sta** … **op**)*

**3 — Mini dialogue**
**A:** Hoe laat ben je klaar? **B:** Om half zes, denk ik.

**4 — Find the odd one**
Which word fits *least* with this unit: **chatgroep**, **broodje**, **trein**? Why?

**5 — True / false**
Say **waar** or **niet waar**: "In Dutch, the verb often moves when you ask a yes/no question."

**6 — Your sentence**
Write one true sentence about yourself using **festival** or **vriendengroep**.

**7 — Transform**
Make this informal line a bit more formal: "Kun je me helpen?" → start with **Kunt u …**

**8 — Quick translation check**
"Ik ga mee naar de film, want ik heb zin." — what does it mean in English (your own words)?
---

**All four skills — finish this lesson with these four micro-tasks**

🎧 **Listening:** Use TTS on this Dutch line (slow, then closer to natural): *Ik ga mee naar de film, want ik heb zin.*

📖 **Reading:** Read silently, then say the **main idea in one English sentence**: *Ik ga mee naar de film, want ik heb zin.*

✍️ **Writing:** Write **one new Dutch sentence** that uses **festival** and fits *Reason clauses: want / omdat + word order*.

🗣️ **Speaking:** Read your new sentence **aloud twice** — exaggerate stress the first time, normal the second.

*(If this lesson was already heavy on one skill, treat that skill as “extra reps” — the point is you always touch all four.)*
steps.teacher_notes: Mix modalities; at least 6 tasks — all concrete above.
